title: Hutt Empire

{{Quote|Millennia ago, we were idolized by the younger species. We brought a golden age to a million worlds.|Szajin|<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]''</ref>}}
The '''Hutt Empire''' was an ancient [[Politics/Legends|empire]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] located in modern [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] ruled by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varl/Legends|Varl]] and was a major [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power [[Pre-Republic era|before]] the [[25,053 BBY|advent]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

The power of the empire was measured primarily in its position as an economic powerhouse, it accomplished this by [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] species it deemed valuable enough to serve it, and exterminating species it deemed undesirable. Though the Empire enforced its will primarily through economic exploitation, it was capable of mustering a tremendous military effort on occasion, especially when its existence was threatened. The most notable use case of this was during the [[Xim Wars|Tionese invasion of the Empire]] by [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]]; where the Hutts [[Third Battle of Vontor|overcame]] the Tionese forces and preserved their domain.

After the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|founding of the Republic]], the Empire is known to have fought alongside the nascent Republic against the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion]] during the [[Tionese War]].

After the devastating events of the [[Hutt Cataclysms]], the Hutt Empire collapsed. Following the devastation of Varl, the Hutts relocated to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], and the remnants of their empire became the oligarchic [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]].

==History==
===Conquests and expansion===
[[File:Hutt_warrior_TEGTW.jpg|thumb|left|190px|A warrior of the ancient Hutts]]
Between the [[25,200 BBY|fall]] of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] and the [[25,053 BBY|rise]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Hutt Empire was the major power in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], although its influence was primarily economic; the Hutts generally seemed to regard political or military conquest as less important than control of commerce.

Despite this, the Hutts could be ruthless warriors when necessary, enslaving those species they considered useful and exterminating those they did not. One of the few species in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] that escaped either of these fates were the [[Sakiyan/Legends|Sakiyans]], who won the Hutts respect and kept their independence in exchange for tribute payments. The histories of ancient Rim worlds like [[Delacrix/Legends|Delacrix]] and [[Rinn/Legends|Rinn]] recorded the fate of planets caught between feuding Hutt clans or offended the Hutts, and had been depopulated, poisoned, bombarded or incinerated.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

===War with the Tion===
{{Quote|To the seventh dungeon with the conquerer-fool. There he shall subjugate rotworms, while his gearwork soldiers stand in glorious tribute to High Exaltedness Kossak.|Kossak Inijic Ar'durv, speaking of Xim|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
[[File:The_Devouring_and_the_Tionese_War.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Hutt Empire during [[the Devouring]] and the [[Tionese War]]]]
One of the greatest expressions of Hutt militarism was the war against [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]]. During Xim's reign his own empire attempted to vanquish the Hutts and rule the [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]], resulting in the [[Xim Wars]]. However, the Hutt Empire made a pact with the [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]], [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]] and [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodrans]]. This new slave army was crucial to the Hutt victory at the [[Third Battle of Vontor]] circa [[25,100 BBY]] which saw the defeat of Xim's [[war-robot]]s.

Following the battle, the [[Treaty of Vontor]] was signed by the Hutts, Klatooinians, Vodrans, and Nikto. This treaty set the Hutts up as eternal overlords of the three other species and of the Si'Klaata Cluster. The Hutt Empire then launched a devastating invasion of the Tionese colonies in the Kiirium Reaches known as [[the Devouring]], which utterly obliterated and depopulated the Tionese empire there, leaving an uninhabited stretch of space that from then on would be known as the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Ash Worlds]]

During the [[Tionese War]] in [[24,000 BBY]], the Hutt Empire alongside the Galactic Republic won its war against the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion]] (one of the feuding states that rose from the ashes of Xim's empire a millennium after his death) by using its agents to stir up trouble in Hutt Space. This worsened the divide between the Hutts and Tionese.

===Decline===
After the devastating civil war known as the [[Hutt Cataclysms]] of [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]], the Hutt Empire collapsed, with their destruction of their homeworld [[Varl/Legends|Varl]], moved to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] and all that was left became known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], with Nal Hutta as the new capital. In the aftermath, the Hutts under the leadership of [[Budhila Hestilic Amura]] established a [[Elders|Council of Elders]] to mediate [[Kajidic/Legends|clan]] disputes and adopted a new philosophy known as [[Kajidic/Legends|''kajidic'']]. This was a pragmatic renunciation of war and conquest, instead advocating indirect control through commerce and, as it developed, the criminal underworld, becoming the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]].

===Legacy===
{{Quote|A long, long time ago, there came a time of revolution, when rebels united to challenge a tyrannical Despot. Leading the rebels was one Kossak the Hutt, who, with trickery, an indentured army, and an Idiot's Array of good fortune, triumphed over Xim the Despot's war-machines at the Third Battle of Vontor.|[[Nal Hutta/Legends|Evocar]]: Episode Three: ''"The Cry of the Evocii"'' by Direus'pei.|<ref name="Xim4">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek05/index.html|text=Xim Week: ''The Despotica'' (Part IV: ''Evocar'')|int=Xim Week: The Despotica}}</ref>}}
The Hutts continued to have influence in the galactic underworld until [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]] during which the Hutt Cartel lost it holdings and influence to the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]] during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|extra-galactic invasion]]. After their defeat in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], the Yuuzhan Vong had left the oversector in anarchy for at least twelve years, but the untouched ''[[Bootana Hutta]]'' world of [[Mulatan]] became the sector's third capital in the aftermath of the war. By [[41 ABY]] it had mostly recovered and joined the [[Confederation]] and thus the Hutts, had once again, dominated the oversector and beyond, at least up until the time of the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|New Galactic Empire]].

==Astrography==
This empire was based mainly in the Outer Rim though it also covered a portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The Hutt Empire was near the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]] and occasionally both fought.

The major trade routes of the Hutt Empire included the [[Pabol Hutta/Legends|''Pabol Hutta'']], the [[Pabol Sleheyron/Legends|''Pabol Sleheyron'']], and the ''[[Pabol Kreeta]]''. The route that would be known as the ''Pabol Hutta'' reached from [[Varl/Legends|Varl]] to [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]]. The ''Pabol Sleheyron'' crossed the ''Pabol Hutta'' at [[Sleheyron/Legends|Sleheyron]] from [[Nimban/Legends|Nimban]] to [[Formos/Legends|Formos]].

==Military==
[[File:Hutt_Armor.jpg|thumb|250px|An ancient armored Hutt warrior.]]
By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], very few records survived of the Hutt Empire's military forces. Ancient [[Sakiyan/Legends|Sakiyan]] chronicles recorded Hutt cavaliers [[Hutt battle armor|armored with iron shells]] and maneuvering via metal limbs, treads or wheels. These would later be compared to the [[Shell Hutts]] of [[Circumtore/Legends|Circumtore]]. Sakiyan tribute-counts also recorded payments for the Sakiyans' assistance in building Hutt ''[[planechanga]]'' - massive tubular [[Railgun/Legends|rail guns]] built to accelerate [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] to [[Planet/Legends|planet]]-destroying velocities.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|In the years before the Republic the Hutts were a dominant force in the galaxy, but they never constructed an empire.|[[Bastila Shan]], [[3956 BBY]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', [[Bastila Shan]] says the Hutts never built an empire. The opposite is stated in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.
